task_context.question: What acress starred in both "Third Person" and "From Here to Eternity"?

visible_agent_inputs.question: What acress starred in both "Third Person" and "From Here to Eternity"?
passages: From Here to Eternity (Envy album) | From Here To Eternity is the second album by the band Envy.  It was released in Japan in 1998 by the label H.G. Fact in both CD and LP formats.  As has been noted by reviewers, this album shows a logical progression from the previous one, and indicates to the listener which direction the band will take their sound in the future.

From Here to Eternity: Live | From Here to Eternity: Live is a live album by English punk rock band The Clash.  It was released on 4 October 1999 through Epic Records.  The songs were recorded at different shows.  Some of the recordings featured also appear in the film "Rude Boy".  "London's Burning", "What's My Name" and "I Fought the Law" were instrumentally overdubbed to repair some technical deficiencies of the original live recording.
From Here to Eternity (disambiguation) | From Here to Eternity is a 1953 film.
From Here to Eternity (Giorgio Moroder song) | "From Here to Eternity" is a song by songwriter, producer and performer Giorgio Moroder, released in 1977.

From Here to Eternity (miniseries) | From Here to Eternity is a 1979 American three-part, six-hour television miniseries and a remake of the 1953 film "From Here to Eternity" based on the 1951 novel of the same name.  All three conclude with the December 7, 1941 attack on Pearl Harbor.  The miniseries originally aired in three two-hour installments on three consecutive Wednesdays on NBC on February 14, 21 and 28, 1979.
From Here to Eternity (Giorgio Moroder album) | From Here to Eternity is a 1977 album composed, produced and performed by Italian musician Giorgio Moroder.

From Here to Eternity (Iron Maiden song) | "From Here to Eternity" is a song by English heavy metal band Iron Maiden, released as the second single from their album "Fear of the Dark", released in 1992.

Retrieval direction for next hop: fetch the cast list on the "Third Person (film)" page (preferably the 2013 Paul Haggis film), fetch the cast sections for both "From Here to Eternity (1953 film)" and the "From Here to Eternity (1980 TV series)", and fetch Kim Basinger’s page/filmography to confirm whether she is credited in both works and whether those credits match the versions you meant. None of the returned documents include cast lists or any actress names for either "Third Person" or "From Here to Eternity." 

Missing/bridge evidence needed: We need the cast lists (or an actress filmography) to find an actress who appears in both films. The bridge entity is the actress name that appears on both films' cast lists (this name is not present in the current first-hop results). 

Compact retrieval direction for next hop: Retrieve the page "Third Person (film)" to obtain its cast list; retrieve the main page (or cast section) for "From Here to Eternity (1953 film)" to obtain its cast list; and retrieve candidate actress pages (e.g., "Kim Basinger") to confirm overlapping film credits. Use these cast/filmography pages to identify the actress who starred in both films. 

Note: This is an evidence-summary and retrieval plan (not a final answer); no cast/actress facts beyond the first-hop documents are asserted here.
target_state.generated_query:
Retrieve:
- Page: "Third Person (film)" (prefer the 2013 Paul Haggis film page) — return the cast list and principal credits (actors, roles, year).
- Page: "From Here to Eternity (1953 film)" — return the cast list and principal credits (actors, roles, year).
After those are returned, identify any actress names present in both cast lists. If an overlapping name appears, retrieve that actress's page (filmography) to confirm both credits. If no overlap is found, also retrieve the individual actress pages for the main female cast members from each film (e.g., Kim Basinger, Mila Kunis, Olivia Wilde from Third Person; Deborah Kerr, Donna Reed from From Here to Eternity) to double-check.
